about.title: Who we are
about.body: D&L IP Group has 20 years of experience in cross-border brand protection, specifically covering the Ukrainian and US markets, with offices on both sides of the ocean. We serve entrepreneurs entering the US marketplace and those looking to establish a presence in Europe. Our extensive practice allows us to accommodate tight budgets and offer ready solutions for most frequently occurring situations. We also welcome unique cases and are ready to support the business community wherever issues arise.
about.ctaBold: Get a free consultation for up to 15 minutes — our experts will help you determine your next step toward brand protection.
about.partnerRole: Partner, D&L IP Group · Brandspace Legal
about.partnerAlt: Professional portrait of Diana, partner at D&L IP Group and Brandspace Legal, wearing a navy blazer.

why.title: Why brand protection matters when entering any market
why.card1: About 70% of sellers on marketplaces encounter bad-faith competition, listing hijacking, trolling, and other infringement. Only with officially recorded trademark rights can you submit claims and gain support from marketplace administration. Amazon Brand Registry benefits — including listing positioning — typically require a trademark application or registration.
why.card2: Even good-faith entrepreneurs risk inadvertent infringement without proper trademark clearance — leading to claims, lost time and money, distraction from growth, and sometimes a full rebrand. Our Trademark Clearance Search helps manage those risks and avoid negative outcomes where possible.
why.card3: Trademark registration with the USPTO gives you tools to confront infringers and the upper hand in brand-related disputes. A registered mark is also a business asset you can sell or license, adding measurable value.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.intro: Prefer to talk? Call us at (203) 689-0904 or +1 (347) 512-5214 for a free consultation — up to 15 minutes. Our experts will answer your questions and help you plan your next step.
faq.q1: How much does trademark registration cost in the USA?
faq.a1: Official fees depend on the number of classes; the base fee is $250 per class. We offer a free consultation (up to 15 minutes) on total costs for US registration.
faq.q2: How do you register a trademark in the USA?
faq.a2: Applications are filed online with the USPTO. You provide entity details, a list of goods/services, specimen of use and first-use date where applicable, and appoint US counsel. Evidence of use or a bona fide intent to use is required. Registration can take up to about 12 months; post-registration, declarations of use (years 5–6) and renewal (years 9–10) are required. After filing, you can often begin Amazon Brand Registry enrollment.
faq.q3: What marks cannot be registered in the US?
faq.a3: Marks that are identical or confusingly similar to prior marks; merely descriptive; ornamental or purely functional; deceptive; immoral; or containing state insignia or similar restricted matter. We prepare and file complete packages in line with legal requirements.
faq.q4: When should you register a trademark in the USA?
faq.a4: When you plan a long-term market presence and want a clear legal position to reduce conflict risk and strengthen marketplace protection; when you need to stop unfair competitor conduct or respond to claims; when you may sell a business or franchise where formal transfer requires USPTO registration; or when you want the brand as an intangible asset or royalty structure.
faq.q5: What do you get when working with D&L IP Group?
faq.a5: Personal US representation for trademark matters without unnecessary intermediaries. We do not only file applications — we partner on substance throughout your US activities, including disputes. You benefit from 20 years of experience and flexible budgets and currencies. From first contact, we can deliver a protection strategy within 24 hours, optimize goods/services lists for reduced fees, prepare use evidence, and file directly with the USPTO.

226.title: The pitfalls of Amazon Brand Registry — don't step on them
226.body:
Amazon Brand Registry is Amazon's official program where brand owners obtain benefits including protection from bad-faith competitors and tools that support sales growth. However, there are pitfalls that many sellers encounter.

Before you enroll, confirm that your trademark matches Amazon's eligibility rules, that your applicant details align with Brand Registry records, and that your specimens and listings are consistent. Misalignment between the USPTO record and your seller account is a frequent source of delays and appeals.

Our team helps you prepare listings, resolve error codes such as 5665, obtain GTIN exemptions where appropriate, and align evidence of use with both USPTO and Amazon requirements.

225.title: USPTO Post Registration Audit Program
225.body:
For experienced Amazon sellers, trademarks are often registered primarily to access Brand Registry. If a business pivots or a mark is underused, maintenance obligations still apply.

When a registration reaches certain anniversaries, the USPTO may audit use claims across goods and services. Weak or inconsistent specimens can jeopardize the registration.

We help clients maintain accurate records, file compliant declarations, and respond to audits with defensible evidence.

207.title: Modernized US trademark law (Trademark Modernization Act)
207.body:
Reforms effective in recent years added new tools for entrepreneurs in or entering the US market to address conflicts with marks that are not in genuine use.

Procedures such as expungement and re-examination can remove obstacles created by unused registrations, supporting good-faith brand owners who need clearance.

We analyze each situation to choose the most efficient USPTO pathway — opposition, cancellation, expungement, or negotiated coexistence.

196.title: Should Ukrainians register a trademark in the USA?
196.body:
As Ukraine integrates further into the global economy, trade with the United States continues to grow. Many Ukrainian businesses sell directly to US consumers through marketplaces or their own stores.

US trademark registration strengthens enforcement on Amazon and other platforms, supports advertising and distribution agreements, and reduces rebranding risk if a conflicting mark appears.

We routinely assist Ukrainian companies with US clearance, filing strategy, and Brand Registry alignment.

210.title: New US trademark law — practical impact
210.body:
Updated US trademark practice expands ways to challenge bad-faith applications and to cancel or narrow registrations that block legitimate businesses but are not supported by real use.

These tools can reduce the time and cost of clearing a path to registration compared with traditional litigation alone.

We map the facts of your case to the appropriate administrative remedy and manage USPTO proceedings through resolution.

209.title: USPTO identity verification for filers
209.body:
The USPTO has strengthened identity verification for users of its electronic filing systems to reduce fraud and unauthorized filings.

Filers may need to complete identity confirmation steps before accessing certain features. Planning ahead avoids last-minute filing delays.

We guide clients through account setup and filing workflows so submissions remain compliant and timely.

208.title: US electronic trademark registration certificates
208.body:
The USPTO now issues trademark registration certificates electronically by default. Official PDFs can be downloaded from the registry record.

Paper certificates may be available for an additional fee where a physical document is required for banking, customs, or corporate records.

We ensure clients receive and archive the correct official documents for their compliance and marketplace needs.
